threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 'bagel' means something from a chain, not a proper one from that little shop by the station. You’ll learn!
Caught the N155 last Saturday and overheard a guy in a bowler hat arguing with a pigeon about bread crumbs. Classic Tooting at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The night I heard a völkerball match break out in the street while kids shouted in three languages. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln can you find a youth club that doubles as an art gallery, hosting open mic nights right next to graffiti-covered walls.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot locals by how critically they assess the taco trucks—no one raves about La Taqueria without noting the new contender on 24th.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Noticed a guy serenading passengers with an accordion on the 38R this morning—had everyone smiling despite the usual grumpy commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the plazas, but it’s the quiet side streets with hidden cafes where the real life happens.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they struggle to figure out the metro system during rush hour—standing in front of the ticket machines like they’re facing a puzzle.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it Dalston Junction instead of 'the Junction'—everyone else knows it’s just the station now.
Last week on the night bus, a guy serenaded everyone with Adele while a dog stared him down. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw the guy selling handmade soap next to a rapper freestyling by the Wutzkyallee, I knew Neukölln had its own rhythm.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln can you find a youth club that doubles as an art gallery one night and a karaoke bar the next. Pure Berlin chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local by their instinct to dodge the Muni when it’s late—only tourists seem surprised when it’s late.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing like the sound of a Muni bus pulling up just as the fog rolls in—classic San Francisco timing. Makes you feel alive, doesn't it?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the hipster cafés, but real locals treasure the small bakeries where you can still smell the bread baking at dawn.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking photos of buildings during siesta; locals know the real beauty lies in the quiet streets at that hour.
